teachingHint: Your child is learning the sounds s, a, t and p. Each letter makes one sound: 's' says sss (like a snake), 'a' says aaa (like the start of 'apple'), 't' says t (a short sharp sound), 'p' says p (a little puff of air). Help your child point to each letter, say the sound, then blend them together: s-a-t → sat. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-a-t → Say each sound: sss, aaa, t. Blend: sat
t-a-p → Say each sound: t, aaa, p. Blend: tap
p-a-t → Say each sound: p, aaa, t. Blend: pat

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! We can't sound this one out. Look at the word and say 'the'. Try to remember what it looks like.

story: A cat sat on a hat. Dad gave the cat a pat!

teachingHint: Your child is learning four new sounds: 'i' says ih (like the start of 'itch'), 'n' says nnn (hum through your nose), 'm' says mmm (hum with lips together), 'd' says d (tongue taps behind top teeth).
